name: Topeka (United States)

colors.symbolism: the hoist panel and the five-pointed star
the main field and the central emblem
the radiant sunflower (the state flower of Kansas)

description: A flag divided vertically into a narrow white panel at the hoist and a larger blue field, with a five-pointed white star on the blue field near the upper fly and a large yellow sunflower (with green leaf and blue inner device) charged across the centre.
history: Topeka's municipal flag combines the Kansas state colours with the sunflower emblem of Kansas and a star marking the city's role as state capital.

features.symbolism: A white hoist panel beside a larger blue field, framing the central emblems.

features.role: five-pointed star on the upper-fly portion of the blue field
features.symbolism: Marks Topeka as the capital of Kansas.
The sunflower is the state flower of Kansas; here it carries an inner blue device suggestive of the city's industries.
